intro: Sphaeropteris intermedia, also known as the intermediate tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia and the Pacific. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeropteris intermedia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit various pharmacological activities.
